spec_match.rationale: The page now names and probes the exact soft-navigation contract: PerformanceObserver entry types, SoftNavigationEntry/implementation aliases, navigationType, interactionId, largestInteractionContentfulPaint, interaction-contentful-paint, PerformanceEntry.navigationId, and performance.getEntriesByType() counts when the native entry types are exposed.

interactive.rationale: The lab exposes route selection, push/replace/traverse navigationType controls, interaction-context and paint toggles, render delay, and explicit URL-only and paint-only failure buttons with live entry JSON and event logs.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The compatibility lab still focuses on support probing and fallback behavior, but now keeps the same-document URL change on the demo route so reloads remain safe.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo directly exercises the spec heuristic failures for missing active interaction context, missing same-document URL change, missing contentful paint, and unavailable native observer support.

references.rationale: The page includes required references to ChromeStatus feature 5144837209194496, the WICG Soft Navigations draft, and Chrome developer documentation.

design_system.rationale: The page uses shared CSS variables for colors, sets color alongside button background overrides, removes inline styles, and avoids raw hex color values.

summary: All prior open questions were addressed in the compatibility lab.
